tldr: Across your 12 live ads, four are sitting on the same 'Only 100 made / British menswear' body copy — and the oldest of those has been running unchanged for 55 days, roughly double the refresh window we'd hold a brand at your scale to. The bigger pattern: zero video in the active set and one scarcity message doing nearly all the talking, which under Andromeda's 2025 retrieval clustering means most of your ads are competing against each other for the same slot.

benchmark.context: Most apparel brands refresh hero creative around the 21-day mark; the top quartile sits closer to 14. You're operating on roughly a 28-day cadence, which is workable for a smaller-volume menswear account — but the 'Only 100 made' carousel that's been live 55 days is well past where Andromeda starts throttling delivery on a recycled concept.

ads.headline: Only 100 made
ads.body: British menswear. Only 100 pieces of each design. Once it's gone, it's gone. Sauce & Brown.
drivers: 55 days live — nearly double your 28-day refresh window and a full month past the apparel category median
Body copy is duplicated word-for-word across four of your live carousels, so Andromeda is treating them as one cluster and only surfacing whichever wins
Headline 'Only 100 made' is recycled across three other ads in your active set — same hook, same scarcity beat, no creative variation

ads.headline: Limited Edition Badge Pack
drivers: 46 days live with one of the thinnest body copies in your active set — 'Don't Miss Out!' is a generic urgency line with no specificity, no benefit, no proof
Lands on a single-product variant URL (?variant=...) which usually under-converts vs. a collection page when the ad creative isn't doing the persuading
Headline tells the viewer what the product is but not why anyone outside your existing buyers would care — no transformation, no story, no hook

ads.headline: The Roots Tee
ads.body: Built for everyday wear. The Roots tee — premium weight, clean fit. Limited to 100. No restocks.
drivers: 42 days live and identical body copy to your other Roots Tee carousel — two ads carrying the same product story for six weeks
Headline is the product SKU, not a hook — works for a returning audience but flat for prospecting
Reuses the same scarcity language ('Limited to 100. No restocks.') that's already running on four other ads, diluting the urgency across the account

ads.headline: Only 100 made
ads.body: British menswear. Only 100 pieces of each design. Once it's gone, it's gone. Sauce & Brown.
drivers: 21 days live and the headline + body are an exact copy-paste of the 55-day-old carousel — the only difference is format (static vs. carousel)
Static format is a useful break from the carousel monoculture, but with the same words it reads as one concept to Andromeda's retrieval engine
At your refresh cadence this lands in the danger band by day 28 — about a week away

ads.headline: Only 100 made
ads.body: British menswear. Only 100 pieces of each design. Once it's gone, it's gone. Sauce & Brown.
drivers: 14 days live but it's the fourth ad in the active set carrying this exact 'Only 100 made / British menswear' body — semantic clustering means it's competing with itself
Same landing page (new-arrivals-1) as three other ads — no LP differentiation to give the algorithm a reason to keep all of them in rotation
Carousel format is shared with five other live ads — your format mix is heavily concentrated and the static break ads aren't different enough to count

hero_concept.hook: Only 100 of these will ever exist. Here's #047.
hero_concept.headline: Worn, not stocked.
hero_concept.primary_text:
Every Sauce & Brown piece gets cut, numbered, and shipped — then we're done. No restocks, no second runs, no 'we're bringing it back next season.'

The Ash Hoodie in vintage washed grey is one of 100. Heavyweight cotton, broken-in hand, the kind of grey that only looks right after a few washes. When the last one ships, that design is finished.

Shop the Ash Hoodie before it's someone else's.
hero_concept.visual_direction: The Ash Hoodie shot flat-lay or on a worn wooden surface in soft window light, with a small numbered hangtag visible — moving the scarcity story from a tagline into a tangible artifact.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Eleven of your twelve ads lead with scarcity as a slogan ('Only 100 made'); none of them prove it visually. This concept turns the claim into something the viewer can see — a numbered piece, not a marketing line — and gives Andromeda a genuinely new concept cluster instead of another rewording of the same headline.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a flat-lay arrangement on a weathered dark wood surface with a small kraft-paper hangtag showing a hand-stamped number placed beside the hoodie, with soft directional north-facing window light raking across the fabric to emphasize the washed cotton texture. Moody, understated British menswear editorial aesthetic — muted, considered, quietly premium. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL — preserve the exact vintage washed grey colorway of the cotton fleece hoodie exactly as shown, with its lived-in, slightly faded heather-grey tone — strictly NO shift to charcoal, black, blue-grey, taupe, brown, or warm-tone bias, NO over-saturating into a flat solid grey, NO removing the washed character of the fabric. STRUCTURE — preserve the exact construction: pullover hoodie silhouette with attached hood, flat drawstrings at the hood, kangaroo front pouch pocket, ribbed cuffs at the wrists, ribbed hem at the bottom, long sleeves, crew-style hood opening. PRESERVE any printed or embroidered Sauce & Brown branding, chest graphic, sleeve hit, or interior neck label exactly as it appears in the reference image — every letter, every color, every placement. Render the product's fabric, weave, color, finish, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, drawstrings, pocket, cuffs, hem, hood, sleeves, branding) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the color temperature, do NOT shift cool grey toward warm tones, do NOT add or remove drawstrings, pockets, zippers, or hardware, do NOT convert the pullover to a zip-up, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify any printed graphics, embroidery, or brand labels on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself — text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.

next_step.urgency: Two of your ads cross into the danger band inside the next nine days.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Sauce & Brown refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Anthony, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep — work with us or don't.

method_note: Built from your 12 live Meta ads (9 with readable copy) plus public Ad Library timestamps; we don't see your CPM, CTR, or ROAS — fatigue is mapped from days-running, copy duplication, format mix, and Andromeda concept clustering.
